title: What defines an Eva in Q
display_title: What defines an Eva in Q
wikitext:
[]: What defines an Eva in Q?             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Thu Oct 09, 2014 11:46 am In the past, it's been pretty clear what the Eva units are--they're humanoid organically-constructed fighting machines cloned from the tissue of either Adam or Lilith, fitted with armor to restrain them. They are controlled by human pilots, or in the case of the Mass Production Evas from EOE, a dummy plug. The thing is, the third movie throws all that out the window. We have the Evangelion Mk. 04 units that attack the Wunder , which look completely mechanical. We see that Mk. 09 and Unit 02 are capable of shapeshifting dramatically, with even their armor changing shape. Even the Wunder seems to be essentially a giant Eva unit. Clearly, the definition of what qualifies as an Eva is not as clear-cut in Rebuild, and especially in Q. Your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Oct 09, 2014 12:49 pm The least headache inducing definition is simple if the show/staff calls it an Eva, it's an Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Thu Oct 09, 2014 1:40 pm I meant more along the lines things like. . .whose souls are in the MK. 04s? Neo-NERV seems to have a huge supply of them, since WILLE mentions being "attacked by them before".

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Oct 09, 2014 1:43 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: The least headache inducing definition is simple if the show/staff calls it an Eva, it's an Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Oct 09, 2014 1:43 pm El Squibbonator wrote: I meant more along the lines things like. . .whose souls are in the MK. 04s?

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Thu Oct 09, 2014 4:33 pm The evas, while still being biomechs are a lot less BIO in rebuild, eva-02 as far as we no is no longer asukas mother and most of the evas seems a lot dumber so at this point eva is just another word for a biomech with a core/at field and requiring LCL fluid to pilot

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Oct 09, 2014 7:09 pm pwhodges wrote: We don't know about any souls in Evas in Rebuild; we even see that pilots can pilot more than one Eva, so the linkage with the mother's soul is absent.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:38 am Don't get me wrong, because I probably am, but are mark IV and the like ever actually referred to directly as Evangelions?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 10, 2014 3:07 am ^ In the theater booklet, the NS are called "Evangelion Mark.04" and Mark.09 as "Evangelion Mark.09", so yes, they are considered Evangelions. Personally I think that during the time skip, "Evangelion" became a catch-all term to design any bio-mechanical weapon with a Core. kuribo-04 wrote: There is that part though in Eva 2.0 where Mari has to change the core of 02 to pilot it.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:22 pm ^ Eva-01 is special regardless of yui because its made out of liliths legs so it might have its own soul too, not sure about the others though

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:40 pm Rosenakahara wrote: ^ Eva-01 is special regardless of yui because its made out of liliths legs so it might have its own soul too, not sure about the others though

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:47 pm ElMariachi wrote: So far it's not confirmed in NTE that EVA-01 was made from Lilith's flesh. We know that Unit 01 is special, since it was the one originally planed to carry on Instrumentality (and is able to do it), so it's probable that it was made from Lilith like in NGE, but it's not confirmed.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:55 pm Again, it's not confirmed in NTE that Rei was pulled out of Unit 01 when a rescue attempt was made for Yui (hell, we don't even know if there was a rescue attempt in NTE, especially since there's the possibility that this time Gendo was in cahoots with Yui), the only confirmed information we have is that after Yui's absorption, her genetic data was used to create the Ayanami Series, nothing more.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Fri Oct 10, 2014 12:59 pm ElMariachi wrote: Again, it's not confirmed in NTE that Rei was pulled out of Unit 01 when a rescue attempt was made for Yui (hell, we don't even know if there was a rescue attempt in NTE, especially since there's the possibility that this time Gendo was in cahoots with Yui), the only confirmed information we have is that after Yui's absorption, her genetic data was used to create the Ayanami Series, nothing more.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Oct 10, 2014 3:18 pm Rosenakahara wrote: Well until FINAL comes out we wont know so assumptions are all we really have at current..........plus since this is eva i imagine that will just raise more questions instead of answering the ones we already have.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Oct 11, 2014 9:34 am I'd just say at sometime during the 14 years Shinji was away NERV found a way to work beyond the original limitations of what made an Evangelion. Now it's all an encompassing term. Technology advances as time moves on. Makes sense that the constraints & rules of the first, second & third generation Eva's no longer would apply to the newest models.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat Oct 11, 2014 1:38 pm kuribo-04 wrote: There is that part though in Eva 2.0 where Mari has to change the core of 02 to pilot it. But that doesn't mean the souls (if there are) are linked to one core, since the core of 05 blew up and Mari can pilot that one. You can (not) expect answers to this in 3.0+1.0.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Oct 11, 2014 2:22 pm Nah; Mari is Shinji's half-sister from an earlier student affair that Gendou had before he met Yui. That's why she's so familiar with him. (Yeah, yeah - wrong thread...)

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Tue Oct 14, 2014 7:04 pm pwhodges wrote: We don't know about any souls in Evas in Rebuild; we even see that pilots can pilot more than one Eva, so the linkage with the mother's soul is absent. The only part of that aspect of Evas we know is Fuyutsuki's statement that Yui and Rei are "in" Unit-01 (and Gendou's calling to Yui when she rejects the dummy plug). Even then, souls are not mentioned, as I recall - in fact, Yui is described as "the control system".

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Oct 15, 2014 12:54 pm But then whose soul(s) would be in the Mk. IVs? They don't seem to have pilot--at least none that I'm aware of, yet they're classified as Eva's nonetheless, implying that they do have functional cores.
